differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis (extrahepatic portal vein obstruction)
Non-cirrhotic portal hypertension (e.g., portal hypertensive biliopathy or idiopathic non-cirrhotic portal hypertension)
Post-traumatic portal vein thrombosis and portal cavernoma
next_steps: Perform urgent cross-sectional imaging with contrast (CT angiography or MR angiography) to evaluate for mesenteric ischemia or progression of thrombus
Perform liver biopsy with specific staining for schistosomal ova
Consult interventional radiology for evaluation of portal venous system patency
Assess for occult chronic infection and parasitic exposure history in Costa Rica
Evaluate for underlying hypercoagulable state (thrombophilia workup)
Monitor for signs of acute mesenteric ischemia given the worsening postprandial pain and weight loss
